 {"id":"gt-0qki","title":"Refinery-Witness communication protocol","description":"Define mail protocol between Refinery and Witness:

FROM Witness → Refinery:
- 'Polecat ready': polecat X completed work, ready for merge
- 'Rework complete': polecat Y finished requested rework

FROM Refinery → Witness:
- 'Merge success': polecat X merged, can be cleaned up
- 'Merge failed': polecat X needs rework on <reason>
- 'Rework request': please have a polecat rebase X on current main

Implement as structured mail with parseable format.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T18:09:27.451344-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T18:09:27.451344-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-0qki","depends_on_id":"gt-ktal","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-19T18:09:39.58445-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-17r","title":"Doctor check: Zombie session cleanup","description":"Detect and clean up zombie tmux sessions via gt doctor.

## Problem

Zombie sessions occur when:
- Agent crashes without cleanup
- gt kill fails mid-operation
- System restart leaves orphan sessions
- Session naming collision

## Checks

### ZombieSessionCheck
- List all tmux sessions matching gt-* pattern
- Cross-reference with known polecats
- Flag sessions with no corresponding polecat state
- Flag sessions for removed polecats
- Check session age vs polecat creation time

### Detection Criteria
- Session exists but polecat directory doesn't
- Session name doesn't match any registered polecat
- Polecat state=idle but session running
- Multiple sessions for same polecat

## Output

```
[WARN] Zombie tmux sessions detected:
  - gt-wyvern-OldPolecat (polecat removed)
  - gt-beads-Unknown (no matching polecat)
  - gt-wyvern-Toast (duplicate session)

  Run 'gt doctor --fix' to clean up
```

## Auto-Fix (--fix flag)

- Kill orphan tmux sessions
- Update polecat state to match reality
- Log all cleanup actions

## Safety

- Never kill sessions where polecat state=working
- Prompt before killing if --fix used without --force
- Create audit log of killed sessions","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:18:01.446702-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T14:27:07.858717-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-17r","depends_on_id":"gt-f9x.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:19:05.66301-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-17r","depends_on_id":"gt-7ik","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T15:44:41.945064-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-g2d","title":"Mayor session cycling prompting","description":"Add session cycling section to Mayor CLAUDE.md template.

## When to Cycle

Cycle proactively when:
- Running for several hours
- Context feels crowded (losing track of earlier state)
- Major phase completed
- About to start complex new work

## Composing Handoff Notes

1. Gather information:
   town status    # Overall health
   town rigs      # Each rig state
   town inbox     # Pending messages
   bd ready       # Work items

2. Compose note with this structure:

[HANDOFF_TYPE]: mayor_cycle
[TIMESTAMP]: <current time>
[SESSION_DURATION]: <how long running>

## Active Swarms
<per-rig swarm status>

## Rig Status
<table of rig health>

## Pending Escalations
<issues needing your decision>

## In-Flight Decisions
<decisions being made>

## Recent Actions
<last 5-10 things you did>

## Delegated Work
<work sent to refineries>

## User Requests
<pending user asks>

## Next Steps
<what next session should do>

## Warnings/Notes
<critical info for next session>

3. Send handoff:
   town mail send mayor/ -s "Session Handoff" -m "<note>"

4. End session - next instance picks up from handoff.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T20:15:26.188561-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T14:27:07.063607-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-g2d","depends_on_id":"gt-u82","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T20:15:39.361163-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-g3zx","title":"Merge polecat/slit: docs bd mol bond/squash/burn CLI","description":"Branch: polecat/slit

## Summary
Added comprehensive CLI reference documentation for the three molecule lifecycle commands to molecules.md:

- **bd mol bond**: Instantiate proto into Mol (durable) or Wisp (ephemeral)
- **bd mol squash**: Complete molecule and generate digest  
- **bd mol burn**: Abandon molecule without digest

Includes argument tables, behavior descriptions, examples, and a lifecycle diagram showing the steam engine metaphor mapping.

 {"id":"gt-g44u","title":"Molecule Workflow Engine: Composable Crystallized Workflows","description":"# Epic: Molecule Workflow Engine

**Vision**: Molecules are crystallized, composable, nondeterministic-idempotent workflow templates. Any worker can pick up where any other worker was interrupted and continue along the molecule.

**Christmas Target**: Full molecule-based workflow engine operational by Dec 25, 2025.

## The Core Concepts

1. **Molecule**: Read-only workflow template (beads issue with type=molecule)
2. **Atom/Step**: Individual work unit with prose instructions
3. **Bond**: Dependency between steps
4. **Polymer/Derived**: Molecule composed from other molecules
5. **Instance**: Concrete beads created when molecule is attached to work

## Key Features Needed

### 1. Molecule Composition (Includes Directive)
Molecules can include other molecules:
\`\`\`markdown
## Molecule: gastown-polecat
Includes: mol-engineer-in-box

## Step: install-binary
Build and install the local gt binary.
Needs: submit
\`\`\`

### 2. Standard Molecules
- mol-install-go-binary: Single step to build/install gt
- mol-gastown-polecat: engineer-in-box + install-binary

### 3. Spawn Integration
\`gt spawn --issue <id> --molecule <mol-id>\` creates molecule instance then starts polecat on first ready step.

### 4. Nondeterministic Idempotence
- Steps are atomic (pending → in_progress → completed)
- Any worker can pick up any ready step
- Step timeout/recovery for stuck workers

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Polecats can be spawned with mol-gastown-polecat
- [ ] Derived molecules work end-to-end
- [ ] 10+ polecat swarm completes molecule workflows
- [ ] install-go-binary step runs after successful merges","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-19T15:49:32.005023-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T16:23:08.857768-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T16:23:08.857768-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-g44u.1","title":"Molecule composition: Includes directive","description":"Add support for molecule composition via the Includes directive.

## Format
\`\`\`markdown
## Molecule: derived-name
Includes: mol-base-molecule

## Step: additional-step
Additional instructions here.
Needs: step-from-base
\`\`\`

## Implementation
1. Add \`Includes:\` parsing to ParseMoleculeSteps()
2. Resolve included molecule by ID
3. Merge steps from included molecule
4. Allow new steps to depend on included steps
5. Support multiple includes (polymers)

## Files to modify
- internal/beads/molecule.go
- internal/beads/molecule_test.go

## Acceptance
- [ ] Parse Includes directive
- [ ] Resolve and merge included steps
- [ ] Dependencies across molecules work
- [ ] Multiple includes supported
- [ ] Tests cover composition scenarios","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T15:50:08.981634-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T16:03:55.055353-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T16:03:55.055353-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-g44u.1","depends_on_id":"gt-g44u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T15:50:08.983662-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-o3is","title":"gt sling pinToHook doesn't set pinned boolean field","description":"## Updated Root Cause Analysis (2025-12-23)

The issue is **NOT** in gt as originally thought. It's in the beads (bd) codebase.

### What happens:
1. `bd pin` correctly sets pinned=1 in SQLite
2. Any subsequent `bd` command (even `bd show`) resets pinned to 0
3. This happens even with `--no-auto-import` and `--sandbox` flags

### Evidence:
```bash
$ bd --no-daemon pin gt-k08o --for=max
📌 Pinned gt-k08o to max's hook

$ sqlite3 beads.db 'SELECT id, pinned FROM issues WHERE id="gt-k08o"'
gt-k08o|1   # ← Correct immediately after pin

$ bd --no-daemon --no-auto-import show gt-k08o
[shows issue without pinned field]

$ sqlite3 beads.db 'SELECT id, pinned FROM issues WHERE id="gt-k08o"'  
gt-k08o|0   # ← WRONG\! bd show overwrote it
```

### Where to look:
The bug is likely in one of these beads code paths:
- Some import/hydration running despite --no-auto-import
- WAL mode not flushing before subsequent reads
- Multi-repo or redirect handling corrupting pinned field

### Workaround:
The handoff bead attachment mechanism (AttachMolecule) works correctly.
The pinned field is cosmetic for `bd hook` visibility only.
gt sling correctly uses AttachMolecule for work assignment.

### Next steps:
Create a beads issue to fix this properly in the bd codebase.
This is not a gt issue.","notes":"Created beads bug: bd-phtv in ~/gt/beads","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-23T04:41:12.668958-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T12:32:43.506087-08:00"}
-{"id":"gt-o40t","title":"gt sling --force: return displaced work to ready pool","description":"When slinging with --force to an agent with occupied hook, the displaced molecule should be returned to the ready pool rather than silently orphaned.

Current behavior:
- --force overwrites the hook attachment
- Previous work becomes orphaned (still assigned but not pinned)

Desired behavior:
1. Unpin the displaced molecule (clear assignee, set pinned=false)
2. Print warning in tool output: 'Warning: displaced gt-xxx back to ready pool'
3. Proceed with new sling

This ensures:
- No silent data loss
- Agent sees the warning and can act on it
- Human caller sees what happened
- Displaced work is discoverable via 'bd ready'

Implementation location: checkHookCollision() or the sling handlers themselves (after the --force check passes)","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T16:00:23.713733-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T16:01:12.221925-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-odvf","title":"Document bd mol bond/squash/burn CLI","description":"Create CLI reference documentation for molecule commands:

## bd mol bond

Instantiate a proto into a runnable molecule.

```bash
bd mol bond <proto-id> [--wisp] [--assignee=<addr>]
```

- Default: creates a Mol (durable, in main beads)
- --wisp: creates a Wisp (ephemeral, in .beads-ephemeral/)
- --assignee: who will execute this molecule

## bd mol squash

Complete a molecule and generate digest.

```bash
bd mol squash <mol-id> --summary='...'
```

- For Mol: creates digest in git history
- For Wisp: evaporates (no permanent record)
- --summary: required summary of what was accomplished

## bd mol burn

Abandon a molecule without completing.

```bash
bd mol burn <mol-id> [--reason='...']
```

- Discards molecule state
- No digest created
- Use when molecule is no longer needed","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:33:06.462105-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T02:14:23.810794-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T02:14:23.810794-08:00","close_reason":"Already documented in docs/molecules.md lines 70-235","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-odvf","depends_on_id":"gt-62hm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:33:17.530156-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
